MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT DOWMAN PRODUCTS, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Katherine Allen, Esq. of Brayton Purcell LLC, (415) 898-1555, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Steven J. Shin, Esq. of Walsworth Franklin Bevins & McCall LLP (415) 781-7072, appearing for Defendant and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted and modified its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for summary judgment granted. Defendant shifts the burden of proof and no triable issues of fact raised. Defendant testified at deposition that not able to identify the product or the specifics of its use. Plaintiff's declaration is admitted per D'Amico. No evidence FixAll contained asbestos. Plaintiff's exposure in the 1970's, no evidence it contained asbestos at the time of plaintiff's use. Ay declaration not admissible - not his area of expertise. Lewis deposition not admissible as irrelevant. Dowd and Wellington depositions not admissible under Evidence Code section 240, no showing of unavailability; and, Evidence Code sections 1220 & 1222.
